meta.title: Local vs API LLM Inference Usage Share by 2028
meta.question: By 2028-12-31, what share of global LLM inference (measured in tokens processed per rolling 30-day window) runs on user-controlled compute â€” on-device plus self-hosted servers operated by the end-user or their organization â€” versus third-party hosted APIs?
meta.resolution: Resolved by the State of AI Report 2028 annual local-vs-hosted inference-tokens breakdown (published typically Oct-Nov 2028). Fallback hierarchy if SotAI does not publish the metric: (a) Stanford AI Index 2029 deployment chapter; (b) Cloudflare AI Radar + HuggingFace Inference telemetry composite; (c) consensus of the three most recent developer surveys (Stack Overflow, State of AI, DevOps Research). Resolved H1 (<15% local), H2 (15-35% local), H3 (35-55% local), H4 (over 55% local) by the most authoritative available figure at resolution date. Robustness rule: if the headline figure falls within 2 percentage points of a hypothesis boundary, require corroboration from at least one fallback source or defer resolution 6 months. 'Local' = user-controlled compute: on-device (phone/laptop/desktop) plus self-hosted servers on infrastructure the user or their organization operates. Explicitly excludes BYO-cloud with third-party model API (that is API). Private-cloud self-hosted open-weights deployments count as local. resolutionDate: 2028-12-31.

tagConfig._docs: SURVEY/BENCHMARK track local share; HARDWARE favors H2/H3; EARNINGS (top-3 API growth) favors H1; REGULATORY (sovereignty mandates) favors H2/H3.

H1.label: Dominant API: local inference under 15% of total tokens
H1.unit: % of global LLM inference tokens running on user-controlled compute
H1.description: Hosted APIs retain decisive dominance. Developer ergonomics, latest-frontier access, and enterprise liability/compliance contracts keep the vast majority of workloads on OpenAI/Google/Anthropic/AWS-Bedrock-style hosted services. On-device inference grows but remains niche (privacy-sensitive / offline / experimentation). Consistent with moat-topic H1.

H2.label: Minority local: 15 to 35% of total tokens on user-controlled compute
H2.unit: % of global LLM inference tokens running on user-controlled compute
H2.description: Self-hosted open-weights deployments at enterprise and mobile on-device inference collectively capture meaningful but minority share. Privacy/sovereignty-driven enterprises go self-hosted; consumer apps embed small models on-device for latency and cost. Hosted API still handles majority of workload.

H3.label: Co-dominant: 35 to 55% of total tokens on user-controlled compute
H3.unit: % of global LLM inference tokens running on user-controlled compute
H3.description: Genuine split emerges. Small/medium models (1B-30B) handle a large fraction of everyday tasks on-device or self-hosted; hosted APIs retain complex reasoning and frontier capabilities. Cost arbitrage drives enterprise split deployment.

H4.label: Majority local: over 55% of total tokens on user-controlled compute
H4.unit: % of global LLM inference tokens running on user-controlled compute
H4.description: Malone's thesis realized. Capability gap closes enough (and local inference cost/latency advantages compound) that most LLM inference runs outside hosted APIs. Implies strong correlation with moat-topic H3/H4 outcomes.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors. Current state (2026-Q1): estimated ~90%+ of LLM inference tokens flow through hosted APIs. 3-year momentum: Apple on-device push (M-series NPUs, Gemini Nano), enterprise self-hosting of Llama/Mistral, sovereignty regulation (EU AI Act). H1 (35%): base rate â€” hosted API infrastructure lock-in plus frontier-capability gap sustains dominance; partial local adoption but under 15% threshold. H2 (38%): modal prior â€” realistic trajectory assuming on-device defaults reach consumer scale for routine tasks AND meaningful enterprise self-hosting. H3 (20%): requires both consumer-scale on-device default AND enterprise self-host majority â€” plausible but requires multiple catalysts. H4 (7%): Malone thesis â€” requires capability parity + cost advantage + enterprise migration all within ~2.5 years. Tail. Priors informed by Bsky thread 2026-04-22 (proptermalone moat debate) and moat-topic priors.

Directional signal toward on-device default for OS AI features. Does not meet t2_apple_on_device_default threshold (requires WWDC/I/O keynote plus developer documentation with no cloud fallback for standard queries). Trade-press pre-confirmation only â€” modest tilt toward H2/H3.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.
No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

posteriorHistory.note: Enterprise engagement with open-weights ecosystem is suggestive but does not equal self-hosting majority share. Modest directional support for H2/H3.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

tier1_critical.desc: State of AI Report 2028 (or 2027) publishes inference-tokens breakdown showing user-controlled compute under 15% of total LLM inference tokens for â‰¥2 consecutive quarterly/half-year updates.
State of AI Report 2028 (or 2027) publishes inference-tokens breakdown showing user-controlled compute over 55% of total LLM inference tokens for â‰¥2 consecutive quarterly/half-year updates.

tier2_strong.desc: Apple/Google ship on-device LLM as default for core OS features (Siri/Assistant/Gmail writing assistance) with no cloud fallback for standard queries â€” confirmed by WWDC/Google I/O keynote plus developer documentation.
Gartner or SotAI enterprise survey confirms â‰¥30% of Fortune 500 run their primary LLM workload on self-hosted open-weights infrastructure (not API).
OpenAI + Google Gemini API + Anthropic combined API token volume grows â‰¥40% YoY through 2027-Q4 (outpacing estimated on-device/self-hosted growth).

tier3_suggestive.desc: HuggingFace reports self-hosted open-weights inference volume (via tracked endpoints + telemetry) at >25% of their ecosystem total for â‰¥6 months.
Major consumer apps (â‰¥3 of: WhatsApp, Instagram, Gmail, Notes, Siri, Gemini app) perform LLM inference on-device by default with opt-in cloud fallback.
EU AI Act or similar regulation mandates local/self-hosted inference for specified sensitive use cases (medical, legal, gov), driving enterprise adoption.

anti_indicators.desc: Authoritative survey (SotAI/Stanford AI Index) reports user-controlled compute â‰¥30% of total LLM inference tokens in any period before resolution â€” contradicts 'dominant API' hypothesis.
Self-hosted open-weights share flat or declining through 2027 per â‰¥2 tracking sources; hosted API share growing â€” contradicts minority-local hypothesis.
Any authoritative source reports user-controlled compute either <20% or >60% of total inference tokens â€” contradicts co-dominant split.
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: H3 -15pp (redistributed to H1+H2 or H4 per direction)

anti_indicators.desc: Top-3 hosted API providers retain combined â‰¥70% of measured inference tokens through 2027 per â‰¥2 independent sources â€” majority-local thesis implausible.

actorModel.description: Usage share between local and API inference driven by: chipmakers (on-device capability), platform vendors (Apple/Google default-inference choices), enterprise buyers (self-hosting decisions), hosted providers (pricing pressure), regulators (sovereignty mandates).

apple_google_platforms.name: Apple / Google (mobile OS)
apple_google_platforms.role: Default-inference gatekeepers â€” their choice of on-device vs. cloud for OS-integrated AI drives consumer-scale share
apple_google_platforms.decisionStyle: Strategic â€” Apple biased to on-device (privacy branding); Google hybrid (Gemini Nano for on-device, hosted for complex). Hardware depreciation cycles drive when new on-device models ship.
overrides: Regulatory pressure (EU AI Act) can force on-device defaults

enterprise_buyers.name: Enterprise buyers (Fortune 500, regulated verticals)
enterprise_buyers.role: Drive self-hosted open-weights adoption for sovereignty/compliance/cost reasons
enterprise_buyers.decisionStyle: Institutional â€” procurement cycles favor multi-vendor strategies; CIO concerns about AI provider lock-in.
filters: Compliance: HIPAA, GDPR, SOC2, FedRAMP require data residency control
overrides: Talent shortage in MLOps can slow self-hosting adoption

hosted_providers.name: OpenAI / Google Gemini API / Anthropic / AWS Bedrock
hosted_providers.role: Defend API share via frontier capability + enterprise features
hosted_providers.decisionStyle: Rational â€” respond to self-hosting threat with price cuts, fine-tuning tools, enterprise agreements.
overrides: Competitive pressure from open-weights can force aggressive pricing

chipmakers.name: Apple Silicon / Qualcomm / NVIDIA / AMD
chipmakers.role: Supply-side â€” NPU/GPU capability determines what's feasible on-device
chipmakers.decisionStyle: Roadmap-driven. On-device AI capability grows ~2x per generation; memory bandwidth is the bottleneck.
biases: NVIDIA: prefers data-center margins (push for API)
overrides: HBM supply crunch can accelerate on-device push (hosted providers can't scale)

regulators_sovereignty.name: EU (AI Act), China (regulatory), US (sector-specific)
regulators_sovereignty.role: Can mandate local/sovereign inference for specific use cases
regulators_sovereignty.decisionStyle: Institutional, precedent-bound. EU already moving on data residency.
biases: Slow to legislate; reactive to incidents
overrides: High-profile AI incident can accelerate local-only mandates

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC â€” only verified deployment data and survey figures move posteriors
WATCH APPLE/GOOGLE WWDC+I/O â€” default-inference choices for OS features drive consumer scale
ENTERPRISE SURVEYS ARE DOWNSTREAM â€” they confirm trends already visible in product launches and hiring
CROSS-CHECK: if moat-2028 posteriors shift toward fragmentation, this topic's local-share priors should rise

evidenceLog.text: Reporting indicates iOS 27 embeds a distilled Gemini Nano (1.3B quantized) directly into the Neural Engine of A17 Pro and M4 chips, replacing the legacy Siri stack with an on-device Apple Intelligence Core. Not yet confirmed via WWDC keynote or developer docs â€” pre-announcement trade press.

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: quorum_failure. Reason: Directional signal toward on-device default for OS AI features. Does not meet t2_apple_on_device_default threshold (requires WWDC/I/O keynote plus developer documentation with no cloud fallback for standard queries). Trade-press pre-confirmation only â€” modest tilt toward H2/H3.

HuggingFace State of Open Source Spring 2026 report: over 30% of Fortune 500 now maintain verified accounts on Hugging Face. Indicates enterprise engagement with open-weights ecosystem but does NOT measure share of primary LLM workload run on self-hosted infrastructure (t2_enterprise_self_host_30pct threshold not met).

GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: quorum_failure. Reason: Enterprise engagement with open-weights ecosystem is suggestive but does not equal self-hosting majority share. Modest directional support for H2/H3.

sotai_local_inference_share.label: State of AI Report: user-controlled compute share of inference tokens
sotai_local_inference_share.unit: % of global LLM inference tokens on user-controlled compute
sotai_local_inference_share.source: State of AI Report (annual, typically Oct-Nov)
sotai_local_inference_share.note: Primary resolution source. If SotAI 2026 does not publish this exact metric, fall back to survey composite.

huggingface_self_host_volume.label: HuggingFace self-hosted inference endpoint volume
huggingface_self_host_volume.unit: tokens/day tracked via HF telemetry + endpoint counters
huggingface_self_host_volume.source: HuggingFace Inference endpoints + Hub download telemetry (indirect)

top3_api_token_volume.label: Top-3 hosted API combined token volume

on_device_llm_shipments.label: Consumer devices shipped with â‰¥3B-param on-device LLM default
on_device_llm_shipments.source: IDC / Canalys smartphone + laptop AI chip shipment reports

watchpoints: State of AI Report annual release (Oct-Nov each year) â€” primary resolution instrument
Stanford AI Index annual â€” fallback resolution source (deployment chapter)
Apple WWDC + Google I/O keynotes â€” default-inference architectural choices for OS AI
HuggingFace ecosystem metrics (self-hosted endpoints, Hub downloads)
OpenAI/Google/Anthropic quarterly API token volume disclosures (where published)
EU AI Act implementation milestones (data residency / sovereignty clauses)
Gartner / Forrester / IDC enterprise AI deployment surveys
searchQueries: State of AI Report local vs hosted inference 2027
enterprise self-hosted LLM vs API 2027 Gartner
EU AI Act data residency LLM

dependencies._docs: Upstream: calibration-local-frontier-inference-2030 (capability parity on smartphones) and calibration-ai-corporate-moat-2028 (API market concentration). Local-frontier H1/H2 imply consumer-scale on-device capability â†’ supports this topic H2/H3/H4. Moat H3/H4 (fragmented/commoditized API market) imply enterprise shift to self-hosting â†’ supports this topic H2/H3. Downstream: none â€” this is a mid-term observable used by both upstream topics' indicators.

upstream.reason: Local-frontier capability is upstream of usage. If smartphones run frontier-class models by 2030, expect usage share to shift toward H3/H4 well before resolution. If local capability stays far behind frontier, H1/H2 dominate.
Moat posteriors proxy enterprise willingness to self-host. Moat H1 strongly implies this topic H1 (API lock-in â†’ low local share). Moat H3/H4 implies this topic H2+.

issues: Stale dependency: calibration-ai-corporate-moat-2028.H2 assumed=0.35, actual=0.5383, drift=18.83%
Stale dependency: calibration-ai-corporate-moat-2028.H3 assumed=0.28, actual=0.0632, drift=21.68%

predictionScoring._docs: Calibration infrastructure. Snapshots are auto-created by engine on each posterior update. Record outcome when topic resolves, then compute_calibration_report() for Brier analysis.
